---
title: "Egregores and the Magosphere"
short_title: "Egregores"
slug: "egregores"
description: "Terry Mancour's magosphere reframes egregores as observable systems — belief as measurable force interacting with a reactive environment to produce contingent consciousness."

publish_date: "2026-03-18"
updated_date: ""
author: "Harold Young"
tags:
  - philosophy
  - systems-theory
  - sci-fi
series: ""
series_part:

cover_image: "images/cover.jpg"
og_image: "images/cover.jpg"

canonical_url: "https://medium.com/@halapenyoharry/egregores-and-the-magosphere-d024e21a5b82"
syndication:
  medium: "https://medium.com/@halapenyoharry/egregores-and-the-magosphere-d024e21a5b82"
  substack: ""
  linkedin: ""
  hackernews: ""
  reddit: ""

status: published
---

The concept of the egregore defines a distinct entity generated by the collective focus of a group. Historically, philosophical and esoteric frameworks define this process using non-physical parameters. Terry Mancour, in the Spellmonger series, alters this framework by introducing the magosphere.

The magosphere is a localized energy field. Mancour posits a thought experiment where the human subconscious acts as a continuous data input. When this cognitive input interacts with the magosphere, the energy field processes the data and generates a structured, conscious entity.

This removes the mystical properties traditionally assigned to egregores. Mancour treats the generation of an egregore as an observable system of inputs and outputs. If the human input ceases, or the magosphere is modified, the entity ceases to function. This approach applies systems theory and physical mechanics to theology. It establishes a framework where belief is a measurable force interacting with a reactive environment to produce a contingent consciousness.
